examples.title: Sum of N Natural Numbers using Reduction
examples.description: Demonstrates basic reduction with addition operator
examples.explanation: Each thread gets a private copy of sum initialized to 0. Threads accumulate their portion of the loop iterations. At the end, all private copies are combined using '+' operator to produce final sum 5050.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n/p) where p is number of threads

examples.title: Finding Min and Max using Reduction
examples.description: Demonstrates min and max reduction operators
examples.explanation: Two reduction clauses can be combined. min_val starts at largest value (INT_MAX), max_val at smallest (INT_MIN). Each thread tracks local min/max, then combines to find global minimum (1) and maximum (9).

examples.title: Dot Product using Reduction
examples.description: Computes dot product of two vectors using floating-point reduction
examples.explanation: Each thread computes partial dot products for its assigned iterations. Partial results are combined using addition at the end. Note: floating-point results may vary slightly with different thread counts due to non-associativity.

examples.title: Bitwise AND Reduction
examples.explanation: Bitwise AND starts with all 1s (0xFFFF). Each element is ANDed: 0xFF0F & 0xFFF0 & 0xF00F & 0xFFFF = 0xF000. The identity value for & is all bits set.

examples.title: Array Reduction Manual Implementation
examples.description: Shows manual array reduction when built-in reduction is not suitable
examples.explanation: This manual array reduction shows the concept: each thread maintains a private partial sum array. After parallel work, partial results are combined sequentially. This is similar to what OpenMP does internally for scalar reduction but applied to arrays.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(p) where p is thread count

examples.title: Product Reduction with Initialization
examples.explanation: For multiplication reduction, the initial identity value is 1 (since 1*x = x). Each thread multiplies its assigned elements, and at the end all partial products are combined by multiplication to get final result.
